title: sempervivum carpathicum
intro: Sempervivum carpathicum, also known as the Carpathian houseleek, is a type of succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sempervivum carpathicum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is also sometimes dried and used as a tea or added to ointments for topical application.
